name: Species
description: Choose your character's species. Your species determines physical traits, special abilities, and the set of game traits you gain from being one of those life forms.

options.name: Dragonborn
options.description: Dragonborn are part of a lineage that stems from a dragon progenitor. Their draconic ancestry grants them a breath weapon and damage resistance.

traits.name: Draconic Ancestry
traits.description: Your lineage stems from a dragon progenitor. Choose the kind of dragon from the Draconic Ancestors table. Your choice affects your Breath Weapon and Damage Resistance traits as well as your appearance.

traits.name: Breath Weapon
traits.description: When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can replace one of your attacks with an exhalation of magical energy in either a 15-foot Cone or a 30-foot Line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in that area must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Constitution modifier and Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, a creature takes 1d10 damage of the type determined by your Draconic Ancestry. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage. This damage increases to 2d10 at level 5, 3d10 at level 11, and 4d10 at level 17. You can use this Breath Weapon a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

traits.name: Damage Resistance
traits.description: You have Resistance to the damage type determined by your Draconic Ancestry trait.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.

traits.name: Draconic Flight
traits.description: When you reach character level 5, you can channel draconic magic to give yourself temporary flight. As a Bonus Action, you sprout spectral wings on your back that last for 10 minutes or until you retract the wings (no action required) or have the Incapacitated condition. During that time, you have a Fly Speed equal to your Speed. Your wings appear to be made of the same energy as your Breath Weapon. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

subOptions.name: Black Dragon
subOptions.description: Your draconic ancestor was a Black Dragon. Your breath weapon and damage resistance are Acid.

subOptions.name: Blue Dragon
subOptions.description: Your draconic ancestor was a Blue Dragon. Your breath weapon and damage resistance are Lightning.

subOptions.name: Brass Dragon
subOptions.description: Your draconic ancestor was a Brass Dragon. Your breath weapon and damage resistance are Fire.

subOptions.name: Bronze Dragon
subOptions.description: Your draconic ancestor was a Bronze Dragon. Your breath weapon and damage resistance are Lightning.

subOptions.name: Copper Dragon
subOptions.description: Your draconic ancestor was a Copper Dragon. Your breath weapon and damage resistance are Acid.

subOptions.name: Gold Dragon
subOptions.description: Your draconic ancestor was a Gold Dragon. Your breath weapon and damage resistance are Fire.

subOptions.name: Green Dragon
subOptions.description: Your draconic ancestor was a Green Dragon. Your breath weapon and damage resistance are Poison.

subOptions.name: Red Dragon
subOptions.description: Your draconic ancestor was a Red Dragon. Your breath weapon and damage resistance are Fire.

subOptions.name: Silver Dragon
subOptions.description: Your draconic ancestor was a Silver Dragon. Your breath weapon and damage resistance are Cold.

subOptions.name: White Dragon
subOptions.description: Your draconic ancestor was a White Dragon. Your breath weapon and damage resistance are Cold.

options.name: Dwarf
options.description: Dwarves are stout folk who often dwell in cities carved into mountains and deep underground. Hardy and stalwart, they are known for their resilience and connection to stone.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 120 feet.

traits.name: Dwarven Resilience
traits.description: You have Resistance to Poison damage. You also have Advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the Poisoned condition.

traits.name: Dwarven Toughness
traits.description: Your Hit Point maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 again whenever you gain a level.

traits.name: Stonecunning
traits.description: As a Bonus Action, you gain Tremorsense with a range of 60 feet for 10 minutes. You must be on a stone surface or touching a stone surface to use this Tremorsense. The stone can be natural or worked. You can use this Bonus Action a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

options.name: Elf
options.description: Elves are a magical people of otherworldly grace, living in places of ethereal beauty. They are part of a lineage that grants them supernatural abilities tied to the Feywild.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.

traits.name: Elven Lineage
traits.description: You are part of a lineage that grants you supernatural abilities. Choose a lineage from the Elven Lineages table. You gain the level 1 benefit of that lineage. When you reach character levels 3 and 5, you learn a higher-level spell, as shown on the table. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose the ability when you select the lineage).

traits.name: Fey Ancestry
traits.description: You have Advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the Charmed condition.

traits.name: Keen Senses
traits.description: You have proficiency in the Insight, Perception, or Survival skill.

traits.name: Trance
traits.description: You don't need to sleep, and magic can't put you to sleep. You can finish a Long Rest in 4 hours if you spend those hours in a trancelike meditation, during which you retain consciousness.

subOptions.name: Drow
subOptions.description: The range of your Darkvision increases to 120 feet. You also know the Dancing Lights cantrip.

traits.name: Enhanced Darkvision
traits.description: The range of your Darkvision increases to 120 feet.

subOptions.name: High Elf
subOptions.description: You know the Prestidigitation cantrip. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can replace that cantrip with a different cantrip from the Wizard spell list.

traits.name: Wizard Cantrip
traits.description: You know the Prestidigitation cantrip. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can replace that cantrip with a different cantrip from the Wizard spell list.

subOptions.name: Wood Elf
subOptions.description: Your Speed increases to 35 feet. You also know the Druidcraft cantrip.

traits.name: Fleet of Foot
traits.description: Your Speed increases to 35 feet.

options.name: Gnome
options.description: Gnomes are magical folk created by gods of the Feywild. They are small, clever, and possess an innate cunning that helps them resist magical effects.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.

traits.name: Gnomish Cunning
traits.description: You have Advantage on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws.

traits.name: Gnomish Lineage
traits.description: You are part of a lineage that grants you supernatural abilities. Choose one of the following options; whichever you choose, Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose the ability when you select the lineage).

subOptions.name: Forest Gnome
subOptions.description: You know the Minor Illusion cantrip. You also always have the Speak with Animals spell prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. You can also use any spell slots you have to cast the spell.

traits.name: Speak with Animals
traits.description: You always have the Speak with Animals spell prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subOptions.name: Rock Gnome
subOptions.description: You know the Mending and Prestidigitation cantrips. In addition, you can spend 10 minutes casting Prestidigitation to create a Tiny clockwork device (AC 5, 1 HP), such as a toy, fire starter, or music box. When you create the device, you determine its function by choosing one effect from Prestidigitation; the device produces that effect whenever you or another creature takes a Bonus Action to activate it with a touch. You can have three such devices in existence at a time, and each one falls apart 8 hours after its creation or when you dismantle it with a touch as a Utilize action.

traits.name: Tinker
traits.description: You can spend 10 minutes casting Prestidigitation to create a Tiny clockwork device (AC 5, 1 HP), such as a toy, fire starter, or music box. When you create the device, you determine its function by choosing one effect from Prestidigitation. You can have three such devices in existence at a time, and each one falls apart 8 hours after its creation or when you dismantle it with a touch as a Utilize action.

options.name: Goliath
options.description: Goliaths are descended from Giants. They are tall, powerful folk who dwell in mountainous regions and value competition and fair play.

traits.name: Giant Ancestry
traits.description: You are descended from Giants. Choose one of the following benefits — a supernatural boon from your ancestry; you can use the chosen benefit a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

traits.name: Large Form
traits.description: Starting at character level 5, you can change your size to Large as a Bonus Action if you're in a big enough space. This transformation lasts for 10 minutes or until you end it (no action required). For that duration, you have Advantage on Strength checks, and your Speed increases by 10 feet. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

traits.name: Powerful Build
traits.description: You have Advantage on any ability check you make to end the Grappled condition. You also count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity.

subOptions.name: Cloud Giant (Cloud's Jaunt)
subOptions.description: As a Bonus Action, you magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

traits.name: Cloud's Jaunt
traits.description: As a Bonus Action, you magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. You can use this a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subOptions.name: Fire Giant (Fire's Burn)
subOptions.description: When you hit a target with an attack roll and deal damage to it, you can also deal 1d10 Fire damage to that target.

traits.name: Fire's Burn
traits.description: When you hit a target with an attack roll and deal damage to it, you can also deal 1d10 Fire damage to that target. You can use this a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subOptions.name: Frost Giant (Frost's Chill)
subOptions.description: When you hit a target with an attack roll and deal damage to it, you can also deal 1d6 Cold damage to that target and reduce its Speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.

traits.name: Frost's Chill
traits.description: When you hit a target with an attack roll and deal damage to it, you can also deal 1d6 Cold damage to that target and reduce its Speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. You can use this a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subOptions.name: Hill Giant (Hill's Tumble)
subOptions.description: When you hit a Large or smaller creature with an attack roll and deal damage to it, you can give that target the Prone condition.

traits.name: Hill's Tumble
traits.description: When you hit a Large or smaller creature with an attack roll and deal damage to it, you can give that target the Prone condition. You can use this a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subOptions.name: Stone Giant (Stone's Endurance)
subOptions.description: When you take damage, you can take a Reaction to roll 1d12. Add your Constitution modifier to the number rolled and reduce the damage by that total.

traits.name: Stone's Endurance
traits.description: When you take damage, you can take a Reaction to roll 1d12. Add your Constitution modifier to the number rolled and reduce the damage by that total. You can use this a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subOptions.name: Storm Giant (Storm's Thunder)
subOptions.description: When you take damage from a creature within 60 feet of you, you can take a Reaction to deal 1d8 Thunder damage to that creature.

traits.name: Storm's Thunder
traits.description: When you take damage from a creature within 60 feet of you, you can take a Reaction to deal 1d8 Thunder damage to that creature. You can use this a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

options.name: Halfling
options.description: Halflings are small, resourceful folk who enjoy the comforts of home but possess a surprising courage and natural stealth.

traits.name: Brave
traits.description: You have Advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the Frightened condition.

traits.name: Halfling Nimbleness
traits.description: You can move through the space of any creature that is a size larger than you, but you can't stop in the same space.

traits.name: Lucky
traits.description: When you roll a 1 on the d20 of a D20 Test, you can reroll the die, and you must use the new roll.

traits.name: Naturally Stealthy
traits.description: You can take the Hide action even when you are obscured only by a creature that is at least one size larger than you.

options.name: Human
options.description: Humans are the most adaptable and ambitious people among the common species. Whatever drives them, humans are the innovators, the achievers, and the pioneers of the worlds.

traits.name: Resourceful
traits.description: You gain Heroic Inspiration whenever you finish a Long Rest.

traits.name: Skillful
traits.description: You gain proficiency in one skill of your choice.

traits.name: Versatile
traits.description: You gain an Origin feat of your choice (see "Feats"). Skilled is recommended.

options.name: Orc
options.description: Orcs are hardy, powerful folk who trace their creation to the god Gruumsh. They are known for their physical strength, endurance, and fierce determination.

traits.name: Adrenaline Rush
traits.description: You can take the Dash action as a Bonus Action. When you do so, you gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to your Proficiency Bonus. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 120 feet.

traits.name: Relentless Endurance
traits.description: When you are reduced to 0 Hit Points but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 Hit Point instead. Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

options.name: Tiefling
options.description: Tieflings are the recipients of a legacy that grants them supernatural abilities tied to fiendish power. They possess an otherworldly presence and innate magical talents.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.

traits.name: Fiendish Legacy
traits.description: You are the recipient of a legacy that grants you supernatural abilities. Choose a legacy from the Fiendish Legacies table. You gain the level 1 benefit of the chosen legacy. When you reach character levels 3 and 5, you learn a higher-level spell, as shown on the table. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose the ability when you select the legacy).

traits.name: Otherworldly Presence
traits.description: You know the Thaumaturgy cantrip. When you cast it with this trait, the spell uses the same spellcasting ability you use for your Fiendish Legacy trait.

subOptions.name: Abyssal
subOptions.description: You have Resistance to Poison damage. You also know the Poison Spray cantrip.

traits.name: Poison Resistance
traits.description: You have Resistance to Poison damage.

subOptions.name: Chthonic
subOptions.description: You have Resistance to Necrotic damage. You also know the Chill Touch cantrip.

traits.name: Necrotic Resistance
traits.description: You have Resistance to Necrotic damage.

subOptions.name: Infernal
subOptions.description: You have Resistance to Fire damage. You also know the Fire Bolt cantrip.

traits.name: Fire Resistance
traits.description: You have Resistance to Fire damage.
